Camping is only allowed on the south shore of Finsevatnet because the lake is the settlement's drinking-water supply; signs at Finse mark where you can pitch. Cross the small dam at the lake outlet near Finsehytta and pick a grassy flat along the shore west of the hut, well away from cabins. Flat-ish, watery, exposed at 1,200m; expect mosquitoes in fine weather and possible snow patches even mid-July. Standard allemannsretten rules, max 2 nights. Bonus: walk back to Finsehytta for dinner and a Finse 1223 beer. [Free | No booking; arrive and pitch. Bring a 4-season-ish setup, nights can hit low single digits in July.]

Name: Finsehytta (DNT staffed lodge)
Description: dnt_hut: Large staffed DNT lodge (sleeps 200+ including overflow mattresses), 300m from the station across the lake outlet. Bunk rooms and dorms, shared showers, drying room, and the full-board package includes breakfast, packed lunch and a 3-course dinner: ideal rider fuel. On-site microbrewery. The closest thing to a campsite-with-services Finse has; there is no official campsite in the village. 2026 summer season opens 26 June. [Full board (bed + breakfast + 3-course dinner + lunch + shower): dorm 1,060 NOK DNT member / 1,390 NOK non-member; rooms 1,200-1,275 / 1,580-1,660 NOK pp. Price list dated 2023, expect modest increases. | Book ahead for mid-July via hyttebestilling.dnt.no, Rallarvegen peak season. DNT members are never turned away even when full (mattress on the floor). A DNT membership pays for itself across a multi-hut trip.]

Name: Hotel Finse 1222
Description: hotel: The historic ~44-room railway hotel on the platform (where parts of The Empire Strikes Back were filmed). Sauna, bike rental and workshop, drying facilities, restaurant and bar. The splurge option after days in a tent. Open through summer and going year-round from 2026. [Roughly 3,000-3,500 NOK per night for two including 3-course dinner, breakfast buffet and packed lunch (recent guests report ~USD 325/night packages). | Book well ahead for mid-July; it is the only hotel at Finse and fills with Rallarvegen cyclists and hikers.]

Name: Flam Bakeri
Description: bakery: Artisan bakery on the quay, steps from the Flamsbana station. Fresh skillingsboller (Norwegian cinnamon buns), bread baked through the day, sandwiches, pastries and Italian-style pizzas. Open every day year-round 08:00-17:00, so it works even on a Sunday. The obvious first stop after rolling off the Rallarvegen descent. [Buns ~NOK 45-60, sandwiches ~NOK 100-150]

Name: Aegir Gastropub (Flamsbrygga, formerly Aegir BrewPub)
Description: pub: Stave-church-styled brewpub on the harbourfront, long-running Tripadvisor #1 in Flam and multiple-time Norway BrewPub of the Year. The Jan 2026 renovation is complete: it reopened rebranded as Aegir Gastropub, one of three dining concepts at Flamsbrygga (alongside Fjord Bistro and Flam Restaurant). Viking-inspired menu with local beer pairings (the Viking Plank concept). Confirmed operating through summer 2026. Book a table in July. [Mains ~NOK 250-400, Viking Plank/beer-pairing menu ~NOK 600+]

Name: Restaurant Arven (Fretheim Hotel)
Description: restaurant: The dining room of the historic Fretheim Hotel, 2nd floor: locally sourced fjord and farm ingredients, modern Norwegian cooking. High-season (May-Sept) evening buffet with fish, shellfish, local meats and desserts, around NOK 695 per person in recent reviews (expect a modest increase for 2026). The best 'proper' dinner in the village if you want to celebrate finishing the route. Reserve ahead in mid-July. [Dinner buffet ~NOK 695 pp (recent seasons)]

Name: Fjord Bistro (formerly Furukroa Kafe)
Description: cafe: Casual bistro-cafe in the harbour courtyard at Flamsbrygga, rebranded from Furukroa Kafe in the 2026 renovation. Lunch, dinner, barista coffee and cakes through the day; menu has included the Flam Burger, Aegir-beer-battered fish and chips, soup and pizzas, plus a summer ice cream counter. Big harbour-view terrace. The cheap-and-fast option when the bakery queue is long; busiest when cruise ships and trains coincide. Showing FIFA World Cup 2026 matches, so confirmed open in summer 2026. [Mains ~NOK 150-250]

Name: Coop Marked Flam
Description: grocery: The village grocery and your resupply point (chain: Coop). A-Feltvegen 18, near the cruise pier and train station, between the playground and the parking lot. Open Mon-Fri 08:00-20:00, Sat 09:00-18:00, and crucially Sun 12:00-18:00 (small-store exemption), one of the few Sunday-open groceries on the whole route. Stocks camping supplies. No alcohol sales on Sundays. [Standard Norwegian grocery prices]

Name: Flamselvi river flats below Berekvam (wild camp)
Description: freecamp: Don't wild camp in Flam itself: the valley floor around the village is farmland and built-up, and locals confirm camping around the village is not tolerated. Instead, stop short on your descent from Myrdal: grassy and gravel flats beside the Flamselvi around Berekvam (the Flamsbana passing-loop station, 343.8m, ~10.5km from Myrdal). This mid-valley stretch is uncultivated gorge between the farms at Kardal above and Hareina below, right on the Rallarvegen road. Keep 150m+ from the station buildings and any cabin, max two nights, leave no trace. Free under allemannsretten. [Free | No booking. Pitch late, leave early; the spot is beside the cycle road so expect passing riders in daytime.]

Name: Flam Camping & Hostel
Description: campsite: Award-winning family-run campsite and HI hostel ~300m from the station, right where the Rallarvegen rolls into the village. ~80 tent pitches on terraced grass (some sheltered, power available, picnic tables and grills), hostel rooms, and cabins with and without bathroom; new sanitary block and kitchen completing for the 2026 season. Explicitly welcomes bikers and hikers; camp kitchen and laundry on site. This is the default bikepacker stop in Flam. [Tent NOK 400/night; hostel rooms from NOK 990 (shared bath) to NOK 1,200 (private); cabins from NOK 1,030 (2BR w/ bath NOK 2,800) | Mid-July is peak season in one of Norway's busiest tourist villages: book hostel rooms and cabins several weeks ahead. Tent pitches are more flexible but arrive by mid-afternoon.]

Name: Fretheim Hotel
Description: hotel: The historic 122-room hotel at the head of the fjord, in business since the 1800s. Not cheap, but the end-of-trip splurge option: fjord-view rooms, big breakfast, Restaurant Arven upstairs, and secure bike storage on request. Recent guests report around NOK 2,800/night for a double; aggregator rates for peak season run higher, roughly USD 340-500. [~NOK 2,800-4,500/night double in July | Book well ahead for mid-July; Flam sells out when cruise and tour traffic peaks.]

Name: Trygstad Bakeri og Konditori
Description: bakery: The classic Roros bakery at Kjerkgata 12, baking since 1906 (not 1948 as often misquoted; official site: 'siden 1906'). Famous for skoleboller and solboller; ideal pre-departure carb load and pastries to pack for the first leg toward Gradalen. Open Mon-Fri 08:30-17:00, Sat 09:00-16:00, and (unusually for Norway) Sunday 11:30-16:30 thanks to Roros' tourist-town status. [30-60 NOK per pastry]

Name: Kaffestuggu
Description: cafe: Roros' oldest eating house (Bergmannsgata 18), cafe-restaurant doing local produce with a modern twist: open sandwiches, sodd, reindeer dishes, good cakes and coffee. Open daily including Sundays (Mon-Wed and Sun 11:00-18:00, Thu-Sat 11:00-23:00). Reliable lunch or early dinner. [Mains ~200-350 NOK]

Name: Vertshuset Roros
Description: restaurant: The best dinner in town: historic inn at Kjerkgata 34 serving regional Roros food (reindeer, local lamb, Rorosmeieriet dairy). Restaurant open Tue 17:00-23:00, Wed-Sat 12:00-16:00 and 17:00-23:00 (kitchen closes 21:30). Closed Sun-Mon, so fine for a Wed/Thu start night. Hours and operation confirmed on the official Roroshotellene page; booking engine live for summer 2026. [Mains ~300-450 NOK]

Name: Skanckebua
Description: pub: Gastropub mid-way up Kjerkgata, run by Bergstadens Hotel, with an upstairs bar overlooking the street. Local meat and fish, burgers (the reindeer burger is a top seller), fish and chips, tacos; no pizza. Open Tue-Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-02:00 (kitchen to 22:00); closed Sun-Mon, so time your pint for Tue-Sat. Confirmed operating via Bergstadens Hotel and Visit Roros. [Mains ~200-450 NOK]

Name: Extra Roros (Coop)
Description: grocery: Main supermarket for resupply, Coop Extra at Havsjoveien 3 on the southern edge of town, on the way out toward the route. Long hours: Mon-Sat 07:00-23:00 and Sun 10:00-23:00 (Roros holds a tourist-place Sunday-trading exemption; Sunday opening confirmed by two listing services). Stock up here: limited resupply until Alvdal at km 163. [Standard Norwegian supermarket prices]

Name: Hittersjoen northeast tip (wild camp)
Description: freecamp: Shoreline at the far northeast tip of Hittersjoen lake, ~2.7 km NE of the town centre: follow Fv31 Brekkveien out of town along the north side of the lake, then lakeside tracks/paths (Borta Sjoa, Malmveien) to the NE end. Flat pine-heath pitches, swimmable lake. This spot is 300m+ from the nearest mapped building; note the east and south shores nearer town are lined with cabins (Sjohaga'an) and a farm (Strommevollen) sits NE of the lake, so do not pitch there. Legal under allemannsretten on uncultivated land, max 2 nights. No facilities; fill water in town. Do not pitch inside the UNESCO town itself. [Free | No booking; allemannsretten rules apply (150m from inhabited houses, leave no trace).]

Name: Roros Camping Moen (ex Haneset Camping)
Description: campsite: The town campsite at Havsjoveien 131, ~2 km southwest of the centre by the Glomma river. Tent pitches plus cabins and motel rooms: basic 4-person cabins from 660 NOK, small 2-person cabins 900-1100 NOK, larger serviced cabins with shower/WC 1190-1680 NOK, single motel room 560 NOK, motel family room 800 NOK (all confirmed on the official site). Reception 09-22, online booking on their site. [Tent pitch 380-500 NOK; cabins 660-1680 NOK | Tent pitches are normally walk-up fine; book cabins ahead for mid-July, Roros is a busy UNESCO destination in summer.]

Name: Rorosvidda Hotell (ex Idrettsparken)
Description: hotel: The budget hotel option, formerly Idrettsparken Hotell (rebrand confirmed by local press and an active 2026 Booking.com listing), at Ora 25, a 4-minute walk from the historic centre and ~500m from the station. Simple rooms with private or shared bath, breakfast typically included, free parking and secure spot for bikes on request. Roughly 1000-1400 NOK per double depending on room type and date (aggregators show from ~$104-132). [~1000-1400 NOK/double | Book 2-4 weeks ahead for mid-July; small property and Roros fills up in summer.]

Name: Vertshuset Roros (rooms)
Description: hotel: Step-up option if you want one comfortable night before 705 km of gravel: historic inn rooms above the best restaurant in town, Kjerkgata 34, breakfast included (served Wed-Sun 07:30-10:00). Part of the Roroshotellene group; booking engine live for summer 2026. [~1600-2200 NOK/double | Book ahead for mid-July; it is the most sought-after address in the historic centre.]

Name: Ellefsplass provisions room (DNT matlager)
Description: mountain_lodge: The hut itself is dinner: self-service DNT cabin with a stocked provisions room (tinned/dried meals, breakfast staples) plus full kitchen, electricity and running water. No staffed meals or meal times; you cook, then register and pay food in the DNT app. Open 1 Jun-10 Sep, so mid-July is fine. Non-guests may buy food without paying the day-visit fee (confirmed in the 2026 price list: payment only required if you also use the common room or kitchen). [Pantry items at DNT list prices, pay in app]

Name: Naerbutikken Tufsingdal (Mauritz Landhandel)
Description: grocery: The resupply for this leg, on the route in Tufsingdal village roughly 20-25 km before Ellefsplass. Address Kopparleden 6075, 2555 Tufsingdalen, phone +47 477 00 083. Chain: Naerbutikken (NorgesGruppen), run by Mauritz Landhandel AS (org 920 776 213, active). Listed access 06:00-20:00 daily (Fri to 22:00) including Sundays via self-service/partially remote-operated shopping; staffed hours are shorter, and the self-checkout may want a Norwegian payment app, so aim for daytime. Also does parcel and medicine pickup. Last shop before the empty Orvdalen-Storlagda-Rendalen stretch. [Normal rural grocery prices]

Name: Jonasvollen Camping kiosk (Femundsenden)
Description: cafe: Lakeside kiosk at the south end of Lake Femunden, departure point for the MS Faemund II boat. Kiosk sales, fishing licences, boat hire and marina. Only worth it as a roughly 9-12 km detour east/southeast from Tufsingdal village for a snack stop and a look at Femunden; it is the only place resembling a cafe anywhere near this leg. The site itself is open year-round per jonasvollen.no, but kiosk hours vary, so call +47 414 03 216 or +47 62 49 89 94 before detouring. [Kiosk prices]

Name: DNT Ellefsplass (self-service cabin)
Description: dnt_hut: The stop itself: a converted farmhouse run by DNT Oslo og Omegn in Oversjodalen (Tolga municipality) with 8 beds, electricity, running water and a full bathroom on the ground floor, unusually plush for a self-service hut. Open 1 Jun-10 Sep annually (confirmed on ut.no). Bedding base provided, bring a sheet liner. [NOK 340 member / 520 non-member (2026 price list, confirmed) | Book ahead for mid-July: only 6 of 8 beds are pre-bookable on hyttebestilling.dnt.no (cabin id 10831), the other 2 are drop-in. Booked beds are released if you have not arrived by 19:00. Pay via DNT app. With 8 beds a booking is cheap insurance even if drop-in usually works on a Thursday.]

Name: Wild camp, south shore of Oversjoen tarn
Description: freecamp: Open heath and pine ground on the south/east shore of the Oversjoen tarn (Kartverket: tjern at 62.2279, 11.4854, Tolga), right beside the road you ride into Oversjodalen from Tufsingdalen, about 3 km northeast of Ellefsplass. Utmark, roughly 800 m from the nearest farm (Oversjoen gard at 62.2344, 11.4831), so comfortably legal under allemannsretten (150 m rule). Lake water for filtering, good swimming. Alternative: pitch beside the hut itself, but the 2026 DNT price list explicitly says campers using the cabin after 18:00 pay full lodging. [Free | No booking. Standard allemannsretten rules: max 2 nights, leave no trace, no open fire 15 Apr-15 Sep.]

Name: Lillemoen holiday house, Oversjodalen
Description: cabin: Fully renovated small farmhouse in the village, about 1 km east of Ellefsplass. Sleeps 5 (two double bedrooms plus a single loft bed), washing machine, dishwasher, full kitchen. Active Airbnb listing ('Lillemoen - Great Nature', Tolga), also promoted on visitnorway.com and roros.no. The comfortable fallback if the DNT hut is full or you want a proper indoor night. [Whole house, rate on Airbnb (not published; expect typical Norwegian whole-cabin pricing well above the DNT hut) | Book on Airbnb in advance; whole-house listings in this area go early for July.]

Name: Jonasvollen Camping
Description: campsite: The nearest official campsite: caravan pitches, tent space and camping cabins at Femundsenden, the south tip of Lake Femunden, with marina, boat hire and kiosk. Open year-round per jonasvollen.no. Off-route, roughly 9-12 km east of Tufsingdal village (so about 25-30 km before Ellefsplass); only makes sense if you want a shorter day 1 and a lakeside night on Femunden rather than pushing to the hut. [Tent pitch price not published; small Norwegian sites typically NOK 150-300. Phone +47 414 03 216 / +47 62 49 89 94 (Kjell Magne) or +47 476 41 937 (Nina Alice) | No advance booking needed for a tent in mid-July; call ahead for a cabin.]

Name: Frich's Spiseri Alvdal
Description: restaurant: Roadside diner-restaurant on Rv3 at Nord-Østerdalsveien 5148, Steimosletta, next to Aukrustsenteret. Verified hours: cafe 08:00-23:00 daily, breakfast 07:00-10:00, dagens middag 12:00-20:00 (Sat to 19:00), a la carte to 21:30 (Sat 20:30). The reliable hot-meal option in Alvdal and open Sundays, unlike most of the town. Phone +47 62 48 80 00. [Mains ~NOK 200-330]

Name: Huldra kafe (Huldra handverk, mat og kultur)
Description: cafe: Craft shop and cafe at Steia 10 in Alvdal centre, confirmed operating: fresh yeast bakes, cakes, open sandwiches, simple hot dishes and popular soups. Daytime hours only (roughly 10:00-16:00, check their Facebook page) and closed Sundays, so it works for a Friday/Saturday arrival but not Sunday 12 July. [Coffee + cake ~NOK 100-150, soup lunch ~NOK 150]

Name: Solan og Ludvigs kafe at Aukrustsenteret
Description: cafe: Cafe inside the Kjell Aukrust centre on Steimosletta, a few hundred metres from Frich's. Verified open daily 10:00-18:00 in July, including Sundays. Light meals, cake and coffee; combine with the Flaklypa exhibit if you have a rest hour. [Light meals ~NOK 100-200]

Name: Extra Alvdal (Coop Extra)
Description: grocery: Main supermarket for resupply, Nord-Østerdalsveien 5152 on the Rv3/Steimosletta strip, 200 m north of Frich's. Coop's own page confirms the store (phone +47 62 48 72 00) but does not publish weekly hours; expect roughly 07:00-22:00 Mon-Sat and verify locally. Closed Sundays under Norwegian trading law, so closed 12 July. Stock up here before the final ~3 km out to Gjelten Bru Camping; next shop on the route is Folldal (~30 km), then nothing until Dombas. [Cheapest chain in town]

Name: Spar Alvdal
Description: grocery: Second supermarket, at Steia 16 in the town centre a few doors from Huldra kafe. Confirmed operating (phone 62 48 76 00); also does hot food and take-away pizza. Closed Sundays. If you do hit Alvdal on Sunday 12 July, fallback food is Frich's (daily 08:00-23:00) or a fuel-station kiosk. [Slightly pricier than Extra]

Name: Gjelten Bru Camping
Description: campsite: The stop itself: riverside campsite at Sølndalsveien 15, ~3.5 km northwest of Alvdal centre and directly on the route toward Folldal/Grimsdalen. Verified: 13 cabins, 45 powered pitches, modern sanitary block, kiosk, TV lounge, playground. Camping season 20 May - 1 Oct; cabins year-round. Coin showers (NOK 10+), washing machine NOK 60, bedding rental NOK 60. Cabin prices confirmed on the official site: NOK 490 (simple 4-bunk, bring sheets/liner), NOK 690 (insulated 2-room), NOK 990 (modern 6-berth with kitchen and own shower/WC). [Tent + 2 riders est. NOK 150-250 (not published, confirm on arrival); cabins NOK 490-990 verified | Site states no reservation needed for tents. For a cabin in mid-July, call/email a few days ahead: +47 973 23 588, post@gjeltenbrucamping.no.]

Name: Wild camp: riverbank pine flats near Gjelten bru
Description: freecamp: Flat pine-and-heath terraces along the riverbank ~400-500 m upstream of the Gjelten bridge, opposite side from the campsite. Caution: this point reverse-geocodes to the Masshaugen farm area (Plassen), so scout on the ground and keep the legal 150 m from any inhabited house or farm building; camp only on open forest utmark, max 2 nights under allemannsretten. River water available (treat it). National open-fire ban applies 15 April - 15 September; use a stove. Realistically only if the campsite is full or you want a free night, since the official site is a few hundred metres away with showers. [Free | No booking; standard allemannsretten rules. Verify the exact pitch on arrival, terrain not desk-verifiable.]

Name: Steien Hotel og Skydsstation
Description: hotel: Restored 1878 coaching inn at Steia 20 in Alvdal centre, the characterful splurge option: 12 unique rooms (21 beds), all with private shower/WC, breakfast included, 2-course dinner for guests from local ingredients, English-inspired garden. Confirmed operating with 2026 prices on Booking.com and Agoda. Phone 975 70 175, post@steienhotel.no. [Approx NOK 1,500-1,900 double incl. breakfast (verify at booking) | Book ahead for mid-July; only 12 rooms, weekends fill.]

Name: Frich's Hotell og Spiseri Alvdal
Description: hotel: Motel-style rooms attached to the Frich's diner at Nord-Østerdalsveien 5148, Steimosletta. Functional rooms with private bath; food downstairs 08:00-23:00 and breakfast 07:00-10:00. The cheapest indoor bed in Alvdal and the practical bad-weather bailout. Confirmed listed on Booking.com, Trivago and aggregators. [From ~NOK 1,100-1,400 double | Walk-in usually fine midweek, but book online for July weekends.]

Name: Grimsdalshytta dining room (DNT staffed hut)
Description: mountain_lodge: The hut itself is the food stop: staffed summer season confirmed 19 Jun-13 Sep 2026 on the DNT English page, so open Sunday 12 July. Three-course dinner served at 19:00 every day (tell the warden on arrival; non-residents and tenters can eat if there is space). Day service 12:00-16:00 confirmed: soup, blings, roemmevafler (sour-cream waffles) and cakes, plus drinks and kiosk items; the hut also does stone-oven-baked pizza. All food made from scratch with local ingredients. Breakfast 190 NOK member / 253 non-member. Note DNT rule (verbatim in the 2026 price sheet): no eating your own food inside the hut, and own stoves cannot be used at the hut even outdoors. [Dinner 470 NOK member / 608 non-member; plate dish 258/300; waffles+coffee ~100]

Name: Grimsbu Turistsenter kafeteria
Description: cafe: Roadside cafeteria at the eastern toll gate of Grimsdalsvegen (route km ~205, the last services before the 25 km climb to the hut). Open year-round; posted hours Mon-Fri 08:00-22:00, Sat-Sun 09:00-19:00 (so open Sunday 12 July, confirm on the day). On-site supermarket/kiosk and guest kitchen too. Honest truck-stop style rather than destination food, but reliable hot meals and the only kitchen between Alvdal and the hut. Phone +47 62 49 35 29. [Cafeteria mains ~150-250 NOK]

Name: Extra Alvdal (Coop)
Description: grocery: Main resupply for this leg: the last full supermarket on route before Grimsdalen, at route km 163 in Alvdal (Nord-Oesterdalsveien 5152). Coop's Extra chain, Mon-Sat 07:00-23:00, and listed Sunday-open 10:00-21:00 (self-checkout format; verify before relying on it for Sunday 12 July). Buy camp dinner/breakfast here if you plan to tent rather than eat at the hut. [Supermarket prices]

Name: Coop Prix Folldal
Description: grocery: Backup grocery in Folldal centre (Dovreveien 2), but it is an 8-9 km off-route detour west of Grimsbu along Rv29 (16-18 km round trip). Mon-Fri 07:00-23:00, Sat 08:00-21:00, closed Sundays, so useless on 12 July. Only worth it if you missed Alvdal and need proper resupply before the valley. [Supermarket prices]

Name: Toftemo Turiststasjon cafe
Description: cafe: Next-morning option on the descent to Dombaas: classic tourist station on the E6 at Vestsidevegen 1, Dovre, about 2 km north of Dovre village and ~9.5 km south of Dombaas (route km ~255). Cafeteria seats 75, a la carte menu plus breakfast/lunch service, homemade food. Good first coffee after rolling out of Grimsdalen. Phone +47 61 24 00 45 to confirm morning hours. [Mains ~180-280 NOK, coffee and waffles less]

Name: Grimsdalshytta (DNT Oslo og Omegn staffed hut)
Description: dnt_hut: 54 beds at 1,000 m between Rondane and Dovre national parks, right on the route at km 230. Staffed summer season 19 June - 13 September 2026 confirmed on the DNT site, so fully operating mid-July. Shower, drying room, food service, card payment. Bed-only rates (incl. shower) per the official 2026 DNT Oslo og Omegn price sheet: dorm 290 NOK member / 382 non-member, 4-6 bed room 445/578, 1-3 bed room 515/670. Full pension (bed, breakfast, 3-course dinner, packed lunch of 4 bread slices) 1,095/1,430 in dorm, up to 1,315/1,710 in 1-3 bed room. Sheet liner required, rent 114 NOK member / 130 non-member; own sleeping bags not allowed as bedding. [Dorm 290-382 NOK bed only; full pension 1,095-1,430 NOK pp (dorm) | Book online at hyttebestilling.dnt.no; mid-July is peak and reserving is sensible, but DNT staffed huts never turn anyone away (floor mattress 186/300 NOK, only offered when all beds taken). The self-service annex (19 beds) closes permanently after Easter 2026 as a wild-reindeer measure; the staffed summer operation continues unaffected. Contact: grimsdalshytta@dnt.no, +47 45 29 81 55.]

Name: Grimsdalshytta teltplass (tent pitch at the hut)
Description: campsite: The hut has a designated tent area (teltplass listed among its 14 facilities on ut.no). 125 NOK member / 160 non-member per person (official 2026 price sheet line: teltere/tilgang til hyttefasiliteter) buys access to hut facilities including shower and toilets. Best-value play for two riders with a tent: pitch here, then buy the 3-course dinner and breakfast inside. Remember you cannot eat brought food inside the hut or run your own stove at the hut itself. [125-160 NOK pp + meals | No booking needed for tenting; pay the warden. Book dinner on arrival (served 19:00).]

Name: Wild camp: Grimsa riverbank flats east of the hut
Description: freecamp: Open heath and grass flats along the Grimsa river, roughly 1.5-2 km east (back) along Grimsdalsvegen from the hut, well over 150 m from the hut and the nearest seter buildings. The Grimsdalen landskapsvernomraade regulation (verneplan for Rondane, vedlegg 3, 122.5 km2) restricts motor traffic and caravan parking but does not prohibit ordinary tenting, so normal allemannsretten applies. Flat pitches, river water, fishing in the Grimsa (licence needed). Free; no facilities. Cook here rather than at the hut. [Free | No booking; standard allemannsretten rules (150 m from inhabited buildings, leave no trace). Move on after a night or two and avoid disturbing grazing animals and reindeer.]

Name: Grimsbu Turistsenter Motell & Camp
Description: campsite: Fallback if you stop short of the hut: full-service campsite at the eastern toll-road entrance, route km ~205 (25-30 km and most of the climb before Grimsdalshytta). Cabins in all sizes (large ones with shower/WC), motel rooms, tent and caravan pitches, cafeteria, on-site supermarket, communal kitchen, sanitary block, bathing lake, playground. Open year-round; listed on camping.no and ut.no. [Tent ~300 NOK for 2; basic cabins from ~600 NOK, en-suite cabins more (confirm on grimsbu.no) | Drop-in fine for tents; phone +47 62 49 35 29 or mail@grimsbu.no for cabins in July.]

Name: Bokhandel'n Bakeri & Mathus (Gjestehus)
Description: bakery: The standout food stop in Vagamo: stone-oven bakery, pizza, burgers and pulled-pork sandwiches in a historic timber bookshop at Vagavegen 45 on the village square, run with Classic Norway Hotels as Bokhandel'n Gjestehus. Google 4.2 (396 reviews), verified. Current listed hours verified: Fri-Sat 10:00-22:00, Sun 12:00-20:00, Mon-Tue 10:00-17:00, Wed-Thu closed (likely extends to daily in peak July, but do not count on Wed-Thu). Confirmed OPEN Sunday, so this is your Sunday 12 July dinner plan. 700 m from Smedsmo Camping. [Pizza/mains ~200-300 NOK]

Name: Cafe Edvard at Ullinsvin
Description: cafe: Summer cafe in the old vicarage (Edvard Munch family connection) above Vaga church, with gallery, historic garden and local-food shop, good for coffee, home baking and snacks. 2026 season verified on ullinsvin.no: Cafe Edvard opens 20 June 2026, summer exhibitions run 21 June to 7 September and are open daily, so it works for a Sunday 12 July lunch. Typical hours 11:00-17:00 (exact 2026 daily hours not yet published, confirm on arrival). 10 min walk uphill from the centre. [Coffee and cake ~100-150 NOK]

Name: Villa Vaga restaurant
Description: restaurant: Restaurant of the village hotel (Classic Norway Hotels), in the centre. Official site confirms a la carte menu in the summer season (the buffet/set-dish service is winter, for hotel guests). The reliable sit-down dinner if Bokhandel'n is full; non-guest dining not explicitly stated, call ahead (+47 99 49 25 35). Operating 2026. [Dinner ~250-400 NOK]

Name: Isbaren i Vaga
Description: cafe: Ice-cream and small-dishes bar at Moavegen 1 in the village centre, dine-in and outdoor seating, active Facebook presence. Seasonal; July daily hours unverified, check Facebook. Backup if closed Sunday: Visit Vaga isbar/Vagais at Vagavegen 51A, a combined tourist office, ice-cream bar (local Vagais factory) and bakery outlet. [Ice cream/small dishes 60-180 NOK]

Name: KIWI Vaga
Description: grocery: Main resupply, KIWI chain, Skogbygdsvegen 2 just off the Rv15 roundabout, 600 m from Smedsmo. Hours verified: Mon-Fri 07:00-23:00, Sat 09:00-21:00, CLOSED Sunday. If you arrive Sunday 12 July, buy Saturday or wait until 07:00 Monday before the Murudalen climb. Second supermarket: Coop Mega Vaga, Vagavegen 47 (Mon-Fri 07:00-23:00, Sat 08:00-21:00, also closed Sunday). Sunday fallback for basics: Circle K Vaga fuel station, Vagavegen 36 on Rv15. Last full supermarket before Beitostolen on this route, so load up for the Vinstre/Bygdin leg. [Groceries]

Name: Smedsmo Camping
Description: campsite: The stop itself: family-run campsite beside Rv15 on the edge of Vagamo (Vagavegen 80), 700 m from the square. Coordinates verified against NorCamp (61.869778, 9.103056). Tent pitches on flat ground, service building with showers, kitchen, washing machines, WiFi. Prices verified on smedsmo.no: tent 230 NOK (280 with electricity), tent + car 320 NOK; 1-room cabin (3-4 pers) 690 NOK + 290 washout, 2-room (4 pers) 790 NOK + 340 washout, flats 1150 NOK + 420 washout. Open through summer (service building closes only Nov-Easter). Tent pitches first-come; cabins for mid-July 2026 bookable now and worth reserving, Vagamo is busy Jotunheimen-gateway territory in fellesferie. [Tent 230-320 NOK (2 riders + tent), cabin 690-790 NOK + washout 290-340 | Tents: no booking needed (first-come). Cabins: book ahead for mid-July at reservations.visbook.com/6978.]

Name: Wild camp: Vagavatnet south shore west of Klones
Description: freecamp: Plausible allemannsretten spot: forested shoreline on the quiet south side of Vagavatnet, west of the Klones farm peninsula (Klones confirmed on the lake shore near the village, reached over Finnbrue or via the side road off Rv15 about 2 km out). Pine/birch woods and lake access; stay 150+ m from buildings and off cultivated fields around Klones. Uncultivated outfield, one to two nights fine; no facilities, take water from the lake or fill bottles in town. Coordinates approximate and geometrically consistent with the south shore; pick your exact pitch on the ground. [Free | No booking; standard allemannsretten rules (150 m from dwellings, max 2 nights, leave no trace).]

Name: Villa Vaga (Classic Norway Hotels)
Description: hotel: The mid-range hotel option: historic village hotel in the centre of Vagamo, 67 rooms, restaurant, bar, indoor pool, sauna, breakfast included. Verified operating 2026 (villavaga.no, Booking.com). Aggregator rates from USD 157-183, so budget roughly 1500-2000+ NOK per night for a July double; the original 1300-1800 estimate was optimistic for fellesferie. [Double ~1500-2000 NOK incl breakfast | Book ahead for mid-July; it is the main hotel in a Jotunheimen gateway town and fills in fellesferie.]

Name: Bokhandel'n Gjestehus
Description: hotel: Guesthouse rooms above the bakery/restaurant at Vagavegen 45 on the square: listed on Booking.com as Bokhandel'n - by Classic Norway Hotels (4-star), family rooms with private bath, garden, restaurant downstairs, 24h front desk. Reclassified from hostel to hotel: this is a small upscale guesthouse, not budget dorm accommodation. Rates about USD 167-250, roughly 1700-2600 NOK. Pricier than Villa Vaga but you sleep above the best food in town. [Room ~1700-2600 NOK | Book ahead for mid-July 2026 via Booking.com; only a handful of rooms.]

Name: Haugseter Fjellstue restaurant
Description: mountain_lodge: The lodge itself is the only food at this stop and it is genuinely good: kitchen built on game, trout from Vinstervatn (the house speciality), local cured meats and stolslapper (mountain pancakes) with jam. Open every day in season (19 June to 27 Sept 2026, confirmed on haugseter.no), restaurant 11:00-20:00, lunch from 11:00, dinner menu 15:00-20:00; call ahead for table reservations. Drop-in travellers welcome (it is the classic road stop on Jotunheimvegen / Mjolkevegen cycle route). Open Sundays, so 12 July is no problem. Breakfast 220 NOK, packed lunch 85 NOK. Generator off 23:00-07:00. [Breakfast 220 NOK, packed lunch 85 NOK (both confirmed); dinner mains not published, budget ~250-400 NOK]

Name: Bygdin sommerkafe (Bygdin Hoifieldshotel)
Description: cafe: Next food on the route, about 11 km past Haugseter where Jotunheimvegen meets Rv51 at the east end of lake Bygdin (NOT 29 km: Haugseter's own site puts it 24 km from Beitostolen, and Bygdin sits 13 km from Beitostolen between them). Summer cafe open 12 June through the first week of October, daily 11:00-18:00: waffles, rommegrot, daily soup, hot and cold dishes and daily dinner plates (children's menu too). Hotel restaurant serves 3-course dinners in the evening (book). Good morning-coffee target if you sleep at Haugseter. [Cafe prices not published; ~100-150 NOK waffles/soup, ~200-300 NOK hot dishes is a reasonable budget]

Name: KIWI 243 Beitostolen
Description: grocery: Main resupply in Beitostolen (Ovrevegen 4A; 13 km down Rv51 from Bygdin, fast descent; ~24 km from Haugseter). Full-size supermarket and, critically, Sunday-open under the tourist-resort exemption: Sundays 09:00-21:00, Mon-Sat 08:00-23:00 (confirmed on kiwi.no, not 07:00 as previously listed). So even if you hit Beitostolen on Sunday 12 July you can resupply. There is no shop of any kind at Haugseter or Bygdin; last grocery before this stop is in Vaga, so carry food for the Murudalen-Vinstre leg. [Normal Kiwi supermarket prices]

Name: Wild camp, Vinstre north shore east of Haugseter
Description: freecamp: Open mountain utmark at ~1,030 m along Jotunheimvegen, about 1-1.5 km east of Haugseter on the north shore of Vinstre: flat heath and grass down to the lake, easy water access, big views. Fully legal under allemannsretten; OSM reverse-geocodes the spot to the Perslettstolen seter area (isolated dwellings only), so pitching 150 m+ from cabins is easy. Exposed above treeline, so stake well. Fishing permits and an evening meal available 15-20 min walk away at Haugseter. [Free]

Name: Haugseter Fjellstue tent pitch
Description: campsite: The fjellstue takes tents on its grounds: 415 NOK per tent per night, shower 69 NOK (both confirmed on haugseter.no; motorhome 525 NOK), restaurant and limited facilities on site. The pragmatic choice: camp cheap, eat the game-and-trout dinner inside. Generator off 23:00-07:00, so charge electronics at dinner. [415 NOK/tent/night + 69 NOK shower | Tent pitches are not bookable online; turning up before the restaurant closes at 20:00 is the practical approach, or call +47 61 34 15 10 to be safe.]

Name: Haugseter Fjellstue (rooms and cabins)
Description: hotel: Family-run staffed mountain lodge on the north shore of Vinstervatn, 60 beds across rooms, apartments and cabins (ut.no confirms 60 beds, betjent). Private, not DNT-run, but ut.no states it gives a discount to DNT members, so bring your card. Room with breakfast 1,429 NOK pp; half board (breakfast, packed lunch, dinner) 1,839 NOK pp; cabins 1,390-2,235 NOK per night plus 319 NOK pp bedding/towels (all confirmed on haugseter.no). Season 19 June to 27 September 2026 confirmed on their own site, so mid-July is squarely in season. [B&B 1,429 NOK pp; half board 1,839 NOK pp; cabins 1,390-2,235 NOK/night + 319 NOK bedding | Book ahead for a bed in mid-July: only 60 beds and it fills with Mjolkevegen cyclists, anglers and Jotunheimen hikers. Phone +47 61 34 15 10 or haugseter.fjellstua@gmail.com (both confirmed).]

Name: Bygdin Hoifieldshotel
Description: hotel: Historic mountain hotel at the east end of lake Bygdin, oldest wing built 1897 (confirmed on bygdin.com history page), ~11 km past Haugseter: the fallback if Haugseter is full or you want to bank a few more km. Summer season early June through the first week of October (their own info page), so mid-July is open. Proper restaurant with 3-course evening dinners, plus the daytime sommerkafe 11:00-18:00. Half-board rates require 2+ night stays (confirmed); single nights room-and-breakfast, bookable direct or via booking.com (2026 rates live, doubles from roughly 1,700 NOK). [Roughly 1,500-2,500 NOK/double B&B depending on room; half board only for 2+ nights | Book ahead for mid-July weekends: +47 61 34 14 00 or booking@bygdin.com (both confirmed on bygdin.com). Listed with 2026 prices on booking.com.]
